prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine computed via DFT (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, tier standard, density-fit) in gas phase with charge 0, multiplicity 1, using PySCF. The calculation analyzed the neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) states to derive Fukui indices f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and the dual descriptor via Mulliken partial charges. All SCF calculations converged; Fukui charge conservation checks (sum f⁺ and sum f⁻) both returned exactly 1.0000, indicating well-converged N±1 states. The nitrogen heterocycle exhibits the highest electrophilicity (f⁺ = 0.4842 on N, atom 1), reflecting its nucleophilic character. The meta and para carbons (atoms 3–4) show the highest nucleophilic Fukui indices (f⁻ ≈ 0.118), consistent with ortho/meta-directing reactivity in pyridine chemistry. Input geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; no geometry optimization was performed (single-point at given structure). Integrity status: trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.48423
